title: baccharoides calvoana subsp. mokaensis
intro: Baccharoides calvoana subsp. mokaensis, also known as the Moka Baccharis, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Asteraceae family native to Madagascar. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Moka Baccharis include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as reducing fever and alleviating pain. Herbal preparations of Moka Baccharis are typically made from the leaves and stems, and can be consumed as teas, infusions, or used in topical applications.
